ideas.title: AI can read your money — but do not fire your accountant
tags: Claude just killed the Finance Industry
ideas.about: Use the Claude finance-agent wave to teach business owners how AI can help them prepare better questions before talking to their bookkeeper, CPA, or financial advisor. The angle is safe, useful, and trust-building: AI is not replacing the expert; it is helping you show up smarter.
ideas.evidence: nocode.joshua's May 16 finance-agent post is the strongest current competitor signal in the scrape: **512,000 views / 24,500 saves / 4.78% save**. His on-screen title was `Claude just killed the Finance Industry`; spoken hook: “Did Claude just kill the finance industry? Claude just released 10 new finance AI agents...” That proves demand, but Braydon should take the more credible mentor angle.
ideas.spokenHook: “AI should not replace your accountant. But it can make your next accountant meeting ten times better.”
ideas.why: It wave-rides the biggest current competitor post while differentiating Braydon from the fear-based “killed finance” frame. Frank understands money, accountants, and better questions immediately. This also fits Braydon's positioning: AI empowers operators and experts instead of replacing judgment.

ideas.title: Claude plus Canva is becoming a design department for one-person businesses
ideas.about: Turn the Claude + Canva connector into a practical local-business demo: flyers, event graphics, social posts, carousel drafts, and brand-consistent assets created from one prompt. Do not frame it as “make pretty graphics.” Frame it as “your tiny business can finally move like it has a design department.”
ideas.evidence: Sabrina has a fresh May 17 Claude + Canva cluster: **41.9K views / 2,934 saves / 7.00% save** with on-screen title `Claude x Canva = $10k / month 🤯`, and **24.9K / 2,006 saves / 8.06% save** with `Claude + Canva is a literal cheatcode 😭😭😭`. Braydon's own Canva post from 10:12 AM has the right raw material: `Claude can now connect to Canva 🤯🤯`, 640 views, 23 saves, 3.59% save while still early.
ideas.spokenHook: “If you make flyers, carousels, menus, or event graphics, Claude just became way more useful.”
ideas.why: This stacks two signals: Sabrina's fresh competitor wave and Braydon's own proven visual-output lane. It should be shot as a visible before/after demo, not a connector announcement. Show the local-business asset appearing on screen.

ideas.title: The one context file that makes Claude stop giving generic answers
tags: This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately
ideas.about: Reframe the underperforming “Claude memory/context” topic around a concrete business asset: one simple context file that tells Claude who you are, who your customer is, what you sell, your tone, your offers, and what good output looks like.
ideas.evidence: Taki's May 11 context-file post is still a strong recent signal at **18.1K views / 775 saves / 4.28% save**, on-screen title `This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately`. Braydon's own Claude memory post underperformed at **2,252 / 1.20% save**, which means the topic is useful but the first version likely did not make the payoff tangible enough.
ideas.spokenHook: “If Claude keeps giving you generic answers, build this one file before you write another prompt.”
ideas.why: This is a V2/reframe, not a lazy carry-over. The new evidence is that Taki's clearer “one file” framing is outperforming Braydon's broader memory framing. Make it a simple asset viewers can copy, not a conceptual explanation of context.

ideas.title: Claude can make the thing, but your taste still matters
ideas.about: Use Braydon's winning image/video posts to teach the expert-in-the-loop principle in plain English. The point is not that AI makes creative judgment irrelevant. The point is that AI gives operators more drafts, more options, and faster iteration — but the human still has to choose what is good.
ideas.evidence: Braydon's two strongest current posts are both creative-output demos: **167.7K / 5.03% save** on Claude image assets and **156.4K / 5.71% save** on cartoon videos. Rachel Woods has the operator-language pattern: “You don't need a human in the loop. You need an expert in the loop.” Her current post is early and small, so use it as language inspiration, not primary proof.
ideas.spokenHook: “Claude can make the asset now. But it still needs someone with taste to know which version is actually good.”
ideas.why: This gives Braydon a booker-friendly thought leadership clip while still tying back to his biggest performing lane. It is not as reach-proven as the finance or Canva ideas, but it deepens the brand and explains why business owners still matter.

ideas.title: Codex mobile is not about coding on your phone
ideas.about: If Braydon wants to revisit Codex mobile, change the frame completely. The value is not “code on a tiny screen.” The value is “send work forward while you are away from your desk.”
ideas.evidence: Braydon's first Codex mobile post is weak at **665 views / 4 saves / 0.60% save**, so this is not a top-priority reach play. But Marcinteodoru has a May 17 Codex-mobile-adjacent hook inside a beginner Claude post, and Braydon's earlier `Claude Code + Codex` post reached **31.3K**. The topic can work only if the use case is operator-clear.
ideas.onScreenTitle: `Codex can work while you are gone`
ideas.spokenHook: “The point of Codex on your phone is not coding on a tiny screen. It is moving work forward before you get back to your desk.”
ideas.why: This is a lower-priority V2. Only shoot it if you can show the workflow visually: issue/task → Codex starts → you walk away → progress happens. Otherwise skip it.

- Best tactical adjustment: every idea should answer, “what does the viewer see on screen in the first 2 seconds?” If the answer is just Braydon explaining, the idea is weaker.

### Hook comparison

| Braydon post | Braydon title/hook | Competitor contrast | Lesson |
|--------------|--------------------|---------------------|--------|
| Canva post | `Claude can now connect to Canva 🤯🤯` / “Claude can now connect to Canva. Watch this.” | Sabrina: `Claude x Canva = $10k / month 🤯` / “make amazing designs, carousels, and social media posts” | Braydon's version is clear but could add a sharper business outcome. |
| Claude memory/context | `Claude: Give Claude a better memory for your project!` / “If Claude keeps giving you generic answers...” | Taki: `This One File Will 10X Improve Your AI Outputs Immediately` | “One file” is more concrete than “better memory.” |
| Codex mobile | `Codex can now work from your phone` / “Most people think you can only use Codex on your computer...” | Better frame: “Codex can work while you are gone” | The phone is not the benefit. Progress while away from desk is the benefit. |
